methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $120M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $300M - $5.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Commonwealth Fusion Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B+

private.name: Pano AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Sage Geosystems
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $400M

private.name: Reverion
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $310M

private.name: CarbonCapture Inc.
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M

data.narrative:
Cemvita Factory, a Series B climate & energy company founded by Moji Karimi, is evaluated against a comp universe of 9 private and 1 public company operating in the same sector. The target carries a current post-money valuation of $120M on $30M total raised to date.

Two triangulation methods apply to this valuation. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $120M Series B post-money valuation, implying $228M. The peer-median method uses five same-stage, same-sector comparables—Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B), Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), and CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M)—to establish a median of $400M, with a range spanning $300M to $5.0B.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $194M to $460M, with a median of $314M. This implies upside of 162% from the current $120M valuation. Key uncertainties include significant dispersion in the peer set, driven largely by Commonwealth Fusion Systems' $5.0B outlier valuation, which suggests heterogeneity in investor conviction across the climate tech sector. The stage-progression method implies more conservative growth, while peer medians suggest material step-up is warranted. The narrow bottom of the range reflects downside risk if Cemvita Factory underperforms relative to moderately valued peers like CarbonCapture and Reverion.
